columns.extraction_plan: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' This indicates two arms. Page 2, under 'Patients and Interventions', further confirms: 'Patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to receive orteronel (300 mg) orally twice daily or bicalutamide (50 mg) administered orally once daily, in addition to continuous ADT.' Thus, there are 2 arms.
